commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: And, A stone of stumbling, and a rock of offense, even to them who stumble at the word, being disobedient: to which also they were appointed. Whereunto also they are set, or placed, i. by God's permission; not that God is the cause of their sins or damnation, (whose will is that every one be saved) but his justice has appointed and decreed punishments against those who, by their own wilful malice, refuse to believe and to follow his doctrine: their stumbling against this stone is wilful and obstinate.

commentaries.author: Tertullian of Carthage
commentaries.content: Was it because Christ was both a rock and a stone? For we read of His being placed "for a stone of stumbling and for a rock of offence."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
